mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that reduces discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in periods of FEDFUNDS decline are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05) or Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in unemployment rate below 5% indicates tightening labor markets, boosting corporate earnings visibility and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in UNRATE < 5% periods are negative or not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in residential construction (HOUST) and real retail sales (RSAFS) signals strong consumer demand, creating positive S&P 500 momentum over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in periods of concurrent HOUST/RSAFS growth are negative or Rank IC < 0.05 over 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL reading above 2.5% combined with FEDFUNDS below 1.0% indicates stagflation risks, causing S&P 500 declines over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in stagflation scenarios are positive or Rank IC > 0.10 in overlapping periods.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above 105.0 signals recovering manufacturing activity, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in INDPRO > 105.0 periods are negative or not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP contraction (GDPC1 < 0) predicts recessionary risks, causing S&P 500 declines over 60 trading days during late-cycle regimes.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in GDPC1 contraction periods are positive or Rank IC > 0.07 in overlapping windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly payroll gain (PAYEMS) above 200k signals strong employment growth, driving S&P 500 positive returns over 5 trading days in expansionary regimes.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in PAYEMS > 200k periods are negative or not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly FEDFUNDS increase above 0.5% combined with CPIAUCSL decline below 2.0% indicates monetary tightening amid disinflation, causing SPX 20d declines.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in tightening/disinflation scenarios are positive or Rank IC > 0.12 in overlapping periods.
